summary: There is insufficient direct evidence from studies comparing minimal access versus traditional surgeries in newborns regarding objective measures of pain; though procedural advantages exist, valid assessment scales are lacking for reliable conclusions. Thus, based solely on available research, one cannot definitively say minimal access surgery results in less pain for infants.
